Can someone explain how to do burst turns with G murphy?
You generate tentacle so your dmg get higher
You stash Waltz cards
On burst turn
You put tentacle in Red
Then exalt with her
And use waltz
You also want to dupe it through pickman / thais exalt or thais posse or other kind of stuff similar to that
So I only exalt on burst turns.
You equip dream of medicine for more of her waltz + 0 cost cards
Not forcibly
If you have OE gmurphy
You OE and stash waltz
They becomes 0 costs
How about her rouse?
And you can use the 2 other 3 costs waltz from dream of medicine covenant to regen tentacle
You want to use it because it convert 50% of dmg done into sacrifice stacks
If you have her wheel + OE + some other factor such as posse and such
The total convertion can becomes like 110% of dmg done converted into sacrifice
Thanks.. yes I do have her OE. I guess im just bad at playing her team. Having issues surviving while waiting for burst. I guess I should just really save waltz on turns when I have to burst.
If you have normal murphy, try to use her with gmurphy
It's quite helpful because exalt reduce all enemies dmg by 50%
And she have weakness +vuln card
That also reduce your own sacrifice stacks by 50%
Is it better than having a full lemurian team? I have faros, miriam, and tulu in the team
It could be better if you're bad at managing sacrifice stacks
It's pretty hard to manage if you don't know how to properly play her
You need to not play her 0 cost
So the aftershock remove sacrifice stacks

big stat stick of a unit

If you want to focus on rushing the story, then going for +12 Blade as early as possible makes sense 
If you want to maximize the rewards from DTide as early as possible, then you'd benefit more from expanding your roster of standard characters and making them usable
Same applies to limited units, rushing the story requires vertical investment, while DTide requires horizontal

Personally, i recommend one in a million, you select Murphy : Fauxborn and you can steamroll the chapters pretty fast if you manage to make her E3.

Others will recommend spreading pulls into getting only 1 of the unit + wheel and then wait for next banner to do the same and use the monthly item from shop to get an universal dupe that can works on limoted units.

Both strat works but have different purposes, 1st strat it's easy story clear but end game content might not have enough core units to beat it ( the end game content need 19 builded unit + 1 borrowed assist unit ).

The other one is to the contrary, you struggle story but can clear the easy levels of the end game content more easily.

Focus on getting some units into E3, then when you reached it go for other realms, don't aim for more dupes because E3 is already enough, you can also pull old limited units through standard banners

ultra isn't necessarily *that* hard, it's more. . . if you like strategy, and thinking about stuff a few turns in advance, then it's pretty easy
Good luck

ultra is about like -- what do you want to have for later, do you want to prepare shields or dps or something else. you can yoink cards out via the black hole if you'd rather delay or need an arithmetica. etc
well thanks for your help guys

Ultra is a 5 turn nuke realm, you setup 5 turns to prepare an extra turn to burst.

You don't get any shield or dmg or heal in between, it rely solely on your units and you kind of need some limited units to make it more enjoyable.

Aequor have more dmg if you switch tentacles into red or shield if you switch it into white

Caro can heal you and give you embryo card that regen alienus or you can choose to devour it

Chaos is more like a support realm that help other realm perform better

it was changed in 2nd anniversary

i wouldnt buy the covenants
um, u can keep buying them but honestly i would just hold onto your scrips
materials are pretty easy to farm later on especially when an event is running

Steppenwolf is what veteran players buy because eventually you will be gated in levels by black and purple gnosis. At that point the rose scrip can be put towards the resource sink that is re-rolling substats

Outside of characters that specifically scale with damage amplification, DR is almost always better 
But it is a breakpoint stat, so do keep that in mind
Wdym breakpoint stat?
Unless you're a gambler, DR has a breakpoint at 100/200/400/800%
Different for arc 2 ruleset, of course
There's no real reason in having 130% DR
since DR gets cut in half every time you trigger, you want to target those numbers

yes
